slides.heading: Presentations that move
slides.sub: Real GPU atmosphere behind every slide — not decoration.

slides.heading: Rendered, not decorated
bullets: Light shafts and height fog raymarched live on the GPU.
Content floats above with real parallax depth.
One shader owns the whole viewport.

slides.heading: Thousands of points, on the GPU

slides.quote: The interface should feel like a material, not a slideshow.

slides.heading: A backdrop that recedes
bullets: Soft morphing gradient — for serious, content-heavy decks.
Motion you feel, not motion that distracts.

slides.heading: Molten, reflective, alive
slides.sub: Procedural metal — normals and fresnel, no textures.

slides.heading: Turbulence, domain-warped
bullets: Three-pass domain warp for fluid billows.

slides.heading: Light through moving water
slides.sub: Iterated phase distortion — the classic caustic kernel.

slides.heading: Drift along the field
bullets: Particles of light advected through an fbm angle field.
Twinkling stars layered behind the gas.

slides.heading: Print, misregistered
slides.sub: Two offset dot channels — the risograph registration error.

slides.heading: Barrel, scanlines, phosphor
slides.sub: Chroma split, subpixel mask, vignette, flicker.

slides.heading: Contours of a living map
bullets: Level lines of an fbm heightfield, slowly drifting.
Elevation tinted from the theme palette.

slides.heading: Glass, on the edge of breaking
slides.sub: Shards displaced by the beat — cracks glint on the pulse.

slides.heading: Pair copy with a product shot
bullets: Text on one side, your image on the other.
Drop in a render, photo, or screenshot.

slides.heading: Design is how it works — not just how it looks.

slides.label: On-device. No frames ever leave the GPU.

slides.heading: Stills and motion, framed in glass
Let's build the new engine
slides.sub: Pick the looks you love — the deck pipeline wires them in.
